name: incorta-upgrade-assistant
description: AI-powered pre-upgrade readiness assistant for Incorta clusters. Validates cluster health, analyses Zendesk tickets and Jira bugs, checks Cloud Portal metadata, tests datasource connectivity, and generates a filled Excel pre-upgrade checklist — all from a single conversation.

tools.name: cloud_portal_connect
tools.description: Authenticate with Cloud Portal via OAuth. Step 1: get a login URL and open it in your browser. Step 2: call again to confirm login and cache the token.

tools.name: generate_upgrade_readiness_report
tools.description: Generate a comprehensive upgrade readiness report combining CMC, Cloud Portal, Zendesk, Jira, and knowledge base data. Outputs a risk rating and Excel checklist data. After the report, call write_checklist_excel to get a download link for the filled Excel file.

tools.name: write_checklist_excel
tools.description: Fill the official Incorta pre-upgrade checklist Excel template with the report data. Pass the checklist_data JSON from generate_upgrade_readiness_report. Returns a direct download URL for the filled .xlsx file.

tools.name: run_pre_upgrade_validation
tools.description: Validate Incorta cluster health before upgrade: services, memory, topology, infrastructure, connectors, tenants, email, and datasource connectivity.

tools.name: extract_cluster_metadata_tool
tools.description: Extract upgrade-relevant metadata from CMC: deployment type, DB type, topology, features, and infrastructure.

tools.name: test_datasource_connections
tools.description: Test all datasource connections on the Incorta Analytics instance and report pass/fail per datasource.

tools.name: get_cloud_metadata
tools.description: Fetch Cloud Portal cluster metadata: Spark/Python versions, sizing, feature flags, consumption, and authorized users.

tools.name: search_upgrade_knowledge
tools.description: Search Incorta documentation and community articles for upgrade guidance.

tools.name: get_zendesk_schema_tool
tools.description: Get the Zendesk support ticket schema available in Incorta for upgrade ticket analysis.

tools.name: query_upgrade_tickets
tools.description: Query Zendesk customer support tickets using Spark SQL for upgrade-related issue analysis.

tools.name: get_jira_schema_tool
tools.description: Get the Jira engineering schema available in Incorta for bug and fix analysis.

tools.name: query_upgrade_issues
tools.description: Query Jira engineering bugs and fixes using Spark SQL for upgrade impact analysis.

cmc_url.title: CMC URL
cmc_url.description: Your Incorta CMC base URL. Example: https://your-cluster.cloudstaging.incortalabs.com/cmc

cmc_cluster_name.title: CMC Cluster Name
cmc_cluster_name.description: The cluster name as shown in CMC under the Clusters page (e.g. customCluster). This is NOT the URL subdomain — it is the internal name configured in CMC.

incorta_analytics_url.title: Incorta Analytics URL
incorta_analytics_url.description: The customer's Incorta Analytics URL — must end with /incorta. Example: https://your-cluster.cloudstaging.incortalabs.com/incorta. Used to log in to Analytics for Zendesk/Jira queries and datasource testing.

incorta_tenant.title: Incorta Analytics Tenant
incorta_tenant.description: Incorta Analytics tenant name (the tenant to log in to on the Analytics instance above)

incorta_username.title: Incorta Analytics Username
incorta_username.description: Username for the Incorta Analytics instance (used for SQL queries and datasource testing, not CMC)

incorta_password.title: Incorta Analytics Password
incorta_password.description: Password for the Incorta Analytics instance (used for SQL queries and datasource testing, not CMC)

cloud_portal_email.title: Cloud Portal Email
cloud_portal_email.description: Your Incorta Cloud Portal email address. Used to authenticate via OAuth and cache your Cloud Portal session.
